claims.claim: CXMT (listing entity Changxin Technology Group, ticker 688825) launched its STAR Market IPO issuance on 2026-07-09 with subscription set for 2026-07-16, targeting RMB 29.5B — the largest A-share IPO of 2026
As of 2026-07-14 no final offer price exists: preliminary inquiry ran 2026-07-13 and the pricing announcement is scheduled for 2026-07-15
The deal structure implies an issue-price valuation floor of roughly RMB 295B (6.688B shares = 10% of post-issue capital raising RMB 29.5B, ~RMB 4.41/share), with a 15% greenshoe to CICC
CXMT moved from SSE acceptance (2025-12-30) to listing-committee approval (2026-05-27) to CSRC registration (2026-06-12) in under six months, the first project under the STAR pre-review pilot
Net income attributable to parent: RMB -8.33B (2022), -16.34B (2023), -7.14B (2024), +1.875B (2025, first full-year profit); accumulated deficit ~RMB 36.65B at end-2025
Q1 2026: revenue RMB 50.8B (+719.13% YoY), consolidated net profit RMB 33.01B, attributable RMB 24.76B (+1,688.3%); H1 2026 guidance: revenue RMB 110-120B, attributable profit RMB 50-57B

CXMT parent directly owns only 30.68% of the Xinqiao fab entity and 31.72% of the Beijing fab entity while consolidating both via acting-in-concert voting agreements; FY2025 minority leakage was ~74% of consolidated net profit, falling to ~25% in Q1 2026
Omdia puts CXMT's global DRAM revenue share at 7.67% in 4Q 2025 and ~7.6% in Q1 2026 (vs ~3% a year earlier), making it the #4 DRAM maker; Counterpoint has ~8% for Q1 2026

SemiAnalysis: CXMT wafer capacity ~265k wspm end-2025 rising to ~350k end-2026 (vs Micron ~385k) and ~420k (~17% of global DRAM capacity) end-2027; bit-shipment share only 9% (2025) to 12% (2027) due to the node gap
Prospectus capacity utilization: 87.06% (2023), 92.46% (2024), 95.73% (2025) — effectively full, so 2026 growth requires new tool move-ins
DDR4 8Gb (1Gx8 3200) spot rose from ~$1.75 (mid-May 2025) to $38.50 session average (2026-07-13), roughly 20x, and had not rolled over as of 2026-07-13
DRAM contract-price growth is decelerating: conventional DRAM +90-95% QoQ in 1Q26, +58-63% forecast in 2Q26, server DRAM +13-18% in 3Q26 (TrendForce)
HBM's share of top-3 DRAM wafer input: ~18% (end-2025) → ~22% (end-2026) → ~30% (end-2027), the structural crowd-out behind the conventional DRAM shortage (TrendForce)

TechInsights teardown: CXMT's 16Gb DDR5 is on the G4 node at ~16nm (D1z-class), 66.99 mm2 die, ~40% larger than Samsung's equivalent; SemiAnalysis estimates cost-per-bit >30% above the Big 3 with G4 yield below the 85-90% mature standard
CXMT LPDDR5X (8533/9600 Mbps) entered mass production in May 2025; CXMT parts are reportedly in >30% of Chinese Android-brand phones ex-Huawei and LPDDR is 66.43% of 2025 revenue

CXMT HBM as of mid-2026: small-scale HBM2 production, HBM3 samples only (delivered to Huawei and partners ~Aug 2025), ~5k wspm HBM capacity end-2025 (<2% of total), HBM3 mass production slipped to 2027, HBM3E targeted 2027; the IPO prospectus contains no HBM capacity-expansion funding line
SemiAnalysis (Sept 2025): CXMT could make only ~2M HBM stacks in 2026, sufficient for ~250-300k Ascend 910C packages, vs Huawei's ~13M stockpiled foreign stacks

CXMT is NOT on the BIS Entity List as of July 2026; Reuters reported (2026-06-16) an interagency committee approved its addition but Commerce has withheld publication, with no Entity List additions since October 2025
Apple approached US Commerce (~late May 2026) seeking assurances to source CXMT DRAM and has begun technical validation of CXMT chips for China-market devices; no commercial commitment
CXMT signed a multi-year server-DRAM supply agreement with Tencent worth more than RMB 20B (~$2.94B) per Reuters (2026-06-29), and has begun taking DDR5 server orders from Tencent and ByteDance
Prospectus: server DRAM rose from 8.39% (2024) to 26.51% (2025) of revenue; top-5 customer concentration 68.08% (2025); revenue outside Greater China only ~2.79%

Valuation ladder: June 2025 private round at RMB 158.4B (Alibaba Cloud RMB 6.1B for 3.85%); IPO floor ~RMB 295B; media/broker post-listing scenarios RMB 1.2-4T (Wang Jiyue: 2-3T at ~20x annualized peak earnings)

The strategic placement is 50.00% of the deal (3.344B shares) — with sponsor co-investment, a 377-employee plan (36-month lock), and unnamed national-level funds; chairman Zhu Yiming pledged a 10-year lock-up and 768M shares for employee incentives

At pre-supercycle prices CXMT lost money at the gross line: 2024 gross margin -4.03% ex-reversals, with product-level gross margins once -108.76% (DDR4) and -121.62% (LPDDR4X); prospectus itself warns H1 2026 growth is at risk of being unsustainable
Bernstein forecasts DRAM prices +43.2% in 2027 then -52.9% in 2028 ($2.23 to $1.05/Gb) as the 2027-29 fab wave (Samsung P4/P5, SK Hynix M15X/Yongin, Micron ID1/ID2, CXMT Shanghai) lands
Seoul prosecutors indicted 10 people (2025-12-26) for leaking Samsung 10nm-class DRAM technology to CXMT; a former Samsung researcher was sentenced to 7 years on 2026-04-22
ASML's China share of net system sales fell from 41% (2024) to 33% (2025) to 19% (Q1 2026), evidence the Chinese DUV stockpiling wave has passed and CXMT runs on its installed base

Micron CBO Sumit Sadana (FQ3 2026 call, 2026-06-24) said CXMT/YMTC have grown in capability and market share but the overwhelming majority of their output is sold within China; CEO Mehrotra expects tight conditions to persist beyond calendar 2027

Consumer demand is already cracking: 3Q26 conventional DRAM contract growth slowed to +13-18% QoQ, DDR4 spot logged its first ~5% decline in about a year, phone OEMs cut memory content and volume targets, and IDC sees 2026 PC units -11.3%
